items.title: 2026 年二季度机械硬盘供给短缺加剧，市场批发价环比上涨 10%
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，全球存储设备短缺危机正全面爆发，且供需失衡局面还将进一步恶化。众所周知，人工智能产业的爆发式增长已造成全球 DRAM 内存芯片与 NAND 闪存芯片严重紧缺，机械硬盘市场同样未能幸免。来自中国台湾地区供应链的消息称，过去一个季度机械硬盘的供货短缺情况持续加剧，硬盘售价也将随之迎来新一轮上涨。据 DigiTimes 报道，2026 年第二季度机械硬盘供应缺口进一步扩大，市场批发价环比上涨 10%。本次涨幅远高于往年同期，根源在于全球各地新建、扩容的 AI 数据中心带来了海量存储需求。报道称，面向普通消费者的 1TB、2TB 机械硬盘依旧货源紧张；而超大规模云服务商大批量采购近线大容量硬盘，使得该品类产品需求量创下历史新高。机械硬盘短缺的本质是供需经济失衡。AI 数据中心需要存储海量数据、图像、视频及各类资源文件，以此支撑云端大模型运行，海量存储需求催生了巨量硬盘采购订单。相较固态硬盘，机械硬盘更适配这类存储场景：其一，单位 GB 存储成本更低；其二，机械硬盘不存在固态硬盘普遍存在的写入次数寿命上限问题。IT之家注意到，暴涨的需求带来了剧烈的市场波动。西部数据与希捷均已证实，自家 2026 全年全部机械硬盘产能，基本都已被 AI 数据中心客户预定一空。受此影响，近半年硬盘整体价格涨幅接近 50%。涨价压力同样传导至消费级硬盘市场。一款西部数据蓝盘 4TB

items.title: AMD "Zen 6" 客户端桌面处理器被曝新增 NPU 但取消 GPU
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，X 平台用户 @Gotou_kai3 北京时间 15 日爆料称，AMD "Zen 6" 微架构主流桌面级 (MSDT) 处理器 "Olympic Ridge" 将集成 NPU 单元但取消核显，引入 CUDIMM 支持，仍无原生 USB4 控制器支持。IT之家注意到，这一爆料如果正确，意味着 "Olympic Ridge" 处理器将采用完全重新设计的 I/O Die 芯片。兼容 CUDIMM 可提升 DDR5 内存频率支持上限，而未集成 USB4 主控意味着主板上的相关接口仍将由外挂芯片提供。倘若 AMD 果真采用 "-iGPU, +iNPU" 的桌面端策略，这意味着该企业一方面在迎合微软的 AI+ PC 策略，一方面也在平衡芯片制造成本：尽管 AMD 的锐龙 AI 400G 系列是首款满足 Copilot 要求的桌面端处理器，但那批芯片本质上还是移动端 APU 的移植版本，不能全面满足多样化的桌面端需求。与此同时，与 "Olympic Ridge" 正面竞争的英特尔 "Nova Lake S" 也将引入高算力 NPU。在引入 NPU 的同时取消核显可节省晶圆面积，降低代工开支。而 AMD 在 AM4 插槽时期推出的原生 MSDT 处理器均无核显，但仍在商业上取得了巨大成功，这也支持了在 "Olympic Ridge" 上的潜在决策。

items.title: 消息称英特尔-英伟达合作 PC 处理器预计 2028Q1 面世
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，X 平台用户 Erdi Özüağ (@fx57) 北京时间昨日爆料称，按照 Intel（英特尔）最新路线图，其与 NVIDIA（英伟达）合作的客户端处理器产品目标 2028 年第 1 季度面世，很可能会在 CES 2028 上发布。根据此前双方透露，在 PC 领域英伟达将向英特尔供应 GPU 芯粒。这一产品可能会被称为 "Serpent Lake" 或一种 "Titan Lake" 变体，是标准 "Razer Lake" 的衍生 / 后继（IT之家注：换句话说，距今有 2 代以上）。@fx57 亦提到，Apple（苹果）与英特尔代工之间的合作磋商仍在持续，谈判的关键点还是在于 Intel 18A 制程工艺的性能与成本表现，当前情况指向 2027Q4 的目标。

items.title: 微软承认 Copilot 按键影响部分用户效率，Win11 将开放重映射功能
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，科技媒体 Windows Latest 今天（6 月 16 日）发布博文，报道称微软承认在 Windows 11 设备上，Copilot 按键并不适合所有人，而且可能会干扰部分快捷键与无障碍工作流。微软本周更新官方支持文档（4 月发布），承认专用的 Copilot 按键会打断部分生产力与无障碍工作流，尤其影响依赖右 Ctrl 键、菜单键或屏幕阅读器的用户。微软表示在后续 Windows 11 版本更迭中，将放宽 Copilot 按键使用方式，支持用户在设置应用中调整该按键功能，调整为右键菜单键或右 Ctrl 键。IT之家援引博文介绍，微软为迎接 Windows 11 AI+ PC（也称 Copilot+ PC）浪潮，于 2024 年联合多家厂商，引入了专门的 Copilot 按键，当时强调它能让用户快速打开 Copilot，提高效率。不过伴随着微软调整 Copilot 战略，该专属按键的定位已逐渐弱化，更多时候只是一个调用 Microsoft Edge 的网页包装器。

items.title: SpaceX 财报仅通过官网与 X 平台发布，不再使用通讯社
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，据路透社报道，SpaceX 在当地时间周一提交的一份文件中表示，公司季度及年度财务业绩、其他重大公告仅会通过官网以及 X 平台官方账号发布，不会通过通讯社发布。此举背离了企业常规信息发布流程。多数企业会借助商业新闻专线（如 Business Wire 或 PR Newswire），面向广大投资者与各类媒体投放公告。SpaceX 称，公司“欢迎投资界人士、媒体及其他相关方关注”其官网投资者关系板块与 X 官方账号，相关披露信息将仅通过上述渠道对外公布。该股周一收盘涨幅约 19%，盘后交易时段涨幅约 2%。当日早些时候，该公司宣布承销商已行使超额配售选择权（绿鞋机制），认购增发股份，使其首次公开募股总募资额增至 857 亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 5803.53 亿元人民币）。埃隆 · 马斯克旗下这家集航天火箭、人工智能与互联网业务于一体的综合企业，此前以每股 135 美元的价格发售 5.5556 亿股，募资 750 亿美元，创下历史募资纪录；即便在承销商行使绿鞋期权前，本次 IPO 规模已是史上最大。

items.title: 苹果统一隐私邮件域名，Sign in with Apple 及 iCloud+ Hide My Email 同步切换
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，科技媒体 MacRumors 今天（6 月 16 日）发布博文，报道称苹果计划统一 Sign in with Apple 以及 iCloud+ Hide My Email 两项功能，统一合并到 private.icloud.com 域名下。IT之家注：Sign in with Apple 是苹果提供的第三方登录方式，用户可用苹果账号快速注册和登录应用、网站，同时隐藏真实邮箱地址，适合重视隐私的场景。而 iCloud+ Hide My Email 是苹果 iCloud+ 提供的隐私功能，用户可生成随机邮箱地址用于注册、订阅或接收邮件，再由苹果转发到真实收件箱。该功能作用是减少垃圾邮件和信息泄露风险，也方便用户在不暴露主邮箱的情况下管理不同服务。Sign in with Apple 目前生成的域名地址使用 privaterelay.appleid.com，而 iCloud+ Hide My Email 功能目前使用 icloud.com 域名，苹果公司宣布今年夏季开始，两者统一迁移到 private.icloud.com。苹果公司表示本次调整不影响现有账号、收件流程和历史邮箱地址，旧地址仍会继续工作，邮件转发也不会受影响。本次调整主要影响开发者和邮件服务提供商。使用 Sign in with Apple 网站和应用需要确保账号系统、邮箱验证逻辑和允许列表

items.title: 苹果 iOS 27 扩展 GymKit 支持 iPhone 17 等，兼容跑步机可同步健身记录
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，科技媒体 9to5Mac 昨日（6 月 15 日）发布博文，报道称苹果扩展 GymKit 健身套件，在 Apple Watch 智能手表基础上，扩展支持 AirPods Pro 3 以及 iPhone 17 等苹果手机。IT之家注：GymKit 是苹果公司推出的健身设备联动功能，此前主要适配 Apple Watch。它能把手表和跑步机、椭圆机等器材连接起来，让设备互相交换训练数据。这样用户不用手动记录，也能得到更完整、更接近真实运动过程的结果。在 iOS 27 系统中，苹果公司扩展 GymKit，用户把 iPhone 贴近兼容跑步机后，就能启动室内步行或室内跑步。跑步机后续回传距离、配速、坡度和热量，AirPods Pro 3 则提供心率数据，整套记录会保存到 iPhone 的 Fitness 应用里。苹果公司强调本次扩展是补充 Apple Watch 的现有体验，AirPods 负责心率，iPhone 充当运动电脑。该媒体编辑在 iOS 27 beta 1 上完成了 16 分钟跑步机步行，记录为 0.66 英里、65 卡路里活动热量、97 卡路里总热量、32 英尺爬升和 115 BPM 平均心率。运动结束后，相关数据会回传到 Fitness 里。

items.title: 华为三折叠“竖折”手机专利现身：修长比例、结构紧凑
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，PostFast 团队与消息源 xleaks7 在挖掘华为专利时发现了一款采用三折叠设计的“竖折”手机，其同步基于专利图制作了手机渲染图。IT之家注意到，目前这项专利还处于申请阶段，暂不确定华为是否会正式投产这款产品。不过从专利图片可以看出，这款手机完全折叠时相当紧凑，在展开后预计将拥有超长“遥控器”级比例。华为在专利里描述，为了能够让天线和内部零件在如此紧凑的机身内正常工作，其设计了一种优化结构层有助于减少手机折叠时的信号损失。作为参考，华为首款三折叠 Mate XT 于 2024 年 9 月上市，匹配一块 10.2 英寸 2232×3184 京东方 OLED 三折屏，展开后风格类似于平板电脑。后续官方又于 2025 年 9 月推出了 Mate XTs 手机，主要升级芯片、新增支持天通卫星通信、引入第二代灵犀通信等。

items.title: 消息称高通洽谈收购 Tenstorrent，旨在提升人工智能芯片研发实力
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，据外媒 The Information 报道，高通正洽谈收购人工智能芯片设计初创企业 Tenstorrent，双方商讨的收购对价区间至少在 80 亿至 100 亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 541.53 亿至 676.91 亿元人民币），相较于 Tenstorrent 上一轮估值存在大幅溢价。作为比较，该公司去年底完成超过 6.93 亿美元 D 轮融资，当时估值约为 26 亿美元（现汇率约合 176 亿元人民币）。目前尚不清楚这笔交易价款是否会包含与业绩目标挂钩的浮动支付款项，以往半导体初创企业收购案通常会设置此类条款。双方谈判仍在进行中，交易金额存在变动可能，洽谈也有破裂风险。若完成收购，高通将大幅扩充自身人工智能与数据中心芯片产品线实力。公开信息显示，Tenstorrent 成立于 2016 年，主要开发用于 AI 模型训练与 AI 应用推理的加速器芯片，由知名芯片设计师 Jim Keller 领导。Jim Keller 在半导体行业拥有极高知名度，曾担任苹果芯片设计师，也曾负责特斯拉自动驾驶芯片开发，被视为业内代表性芯片架构专家之一。

items.title: Anthropic 高管与美政府展开谈判，寻求解除 AI 模型 Fable 5 出口禁令
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，多名政府官员、一名知晓谈判内情人士及一名接近该公司的消息人士向 POLITICO 网站透露，Anthropic 公司员工于当地时间周一与特朗普政府高级官员举行了面对面会谈。此前，美国联邦政府出台出口禁令，迫使这家人工智能初创企业在上周五晚间将其最新模型下架。一名白宫高级官员表示，想要拿出能解除政府上周五管控措施的解决方案，耗时大概率不止短短几天。此次禁令禁止 Anthropic 向境外用户开放其最新大模型，理由是该模型存在潜在安全漏洞。不过这名官员并未彻底关上快速解决争端的大门。“这件事主动权掌握在 Anthropic 手中。”该官员称。知情官员表示，上周末，Anthropic 联合创始人汤姆 · 布朗、美国商务部长霍华德 · 卢特尼克与国家网络总监肖恩 · 凯恩斯克罗斯开展了多轮数小时的通话，随后公司迅速派遣一批具备技术研发与安全防护专业背景的高层前往华盛顿。本文中所有接受采访的消息人士均获准匿名披露谈判细节，Anthropic 公共政策主管萨拉 · 赫克也参与了上述通话。一名熟悉会谈内容的人士透露，周一由美国商务部与美国国家网络总监办公室牵头开展的线下会议侧重技术层面，由相关工作人员主持，美国商务部人工智能标准与创新中心负责人克里斯 · 福尔亦参与其中。一名美国政府官员称，Anthropic 向政府官员做专项汇报，详细介绍了公司的网络安全防护机制

items.title: 承销商全额行使超额配售权，SpaceX 史上最大 IPO 募资总额达 857 亿美元
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，SpaceX 具有里程碑意义的首次公开募股（IPO）规模大幅扩大，此次承销商全额行使超额配售权，认购上限额度股票，募资总额由此增至 857 亿美元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 5801.15 亿元人民币）。埃隆 · 马斯克旗下这家航天与人工智能企业最初募资 750 亿美元，该规模已是史上最高 IPO 募资额。SpaceX 表示，本次 IPO 募集资金将用于多项业务布局。公司计划偿还约 200 亿美元存量债务，这些债务源自社交媒体平台 X（原推特）与马斯克旗下人工智能企业 xAI 的历史贷款；两家企业均在本次上市前并入 SpaceX 体系。募集资金还将用于扩建 SpaceX 人工智能算力基础设施、升级火箭发射配套设施，以及迭代星链（Starlink）卫星互联网业务。SpaceX 股票已于上周五在纳斯达克交易所挂牌交易。当日收盘后，公司总估值突破 2 万亿美元，马斯克也由此成为全球首位万亿富豪。周一该股股价再度走高，推动 SpaceX 总估值超越芯片制造商台积电。

items.title: Adobe 升级 Photoshop、Premiere 等，为用户创作整合更多 AI 能力

items.title: 苹果 iOS 27 优化低电量模式，运行更流畅
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，iOS 27 搭载了大量全新功能，同时为 iPhone 全系统带来核心性能优化，低电量模式下同样生效。苹果将在 iOS 27 中推出了诸多新功能，例如液态玻璃风格滑动控件，以及 CarPlay、苹果钱包、信息、备忘录等应用的升级。除此之外，苹果今年还投入大量精力，通过修复漏洞、优化运行性能，让 iOS 27 的整体使用体验更为流畅。苹果在全球开发者大会主题演讲中重点公布了多项提速优化：应用启动速度最高提升 30%，相册新拍摄素材加载速度最高提升 70%，隔空投送（AirDrop）传输速度最高提升 80%。本次全面性能升级也惠及了低电量模式。苹果在展示数百项 iOS 27 更新内容的演示幻灯片中，列出了两项与低电量模式相关的改动：1. 低电量模式下相机启动速度更快2. 低电量模式下相机功耗更低而实际优化效果不止于此，整机运行流畅度也得到提升。本杰明 · 梅奥在社交平台 X 上针对 iOS 27 首个测试版给出了这样的评价：外媒 9to5mac 也表示，实际体验下来也发现，iOS 27 的低电量模式运行速度明显更快。综合各路网友反馈来看，提速效果因机型而异。部分使用老旧款 iPhone 的用户表示，低电量模式依旧存在明显卡顿，新款机型的优化感受则更为突出。IT之家注意到，目前 iOS 27 仅推出首个测试版，在后续测试周期里，性能优化还会持续迭代。从早期

items.title: AMD 收购 MEXT，为 AI 负载吃内存寻找降本新路径
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，AMD 公司昨日（6 月 15 日）发布公告，宣布收购内存优化技术公司 MEXT，目标是缓解数据中心日益突出的内存瓶颈。IT之家援引博文介绍，在 AI、数据分析、虚拟化和高性能计算负载持续膨胀后，现代数据中心普遍面临内存资源紧张的问题。对云服务商和企业客户来说，内存已成为影响性能、效率和部署速度的重要约束。MEXT 公司主要技术优势在于把闪存扩展为应用可用的“近内存层”，该公司通过内存分层方案，将不常访问的数据从 DRAM 转移到 NAND 闪存。由于闪存单位容量成本远低于 DRAM，这种架构能在不大幅增加主内存投入的前提下，扩大可用内存池规模。MEXT 的预测性内存引擎会持续分析内存访问模式，并借助 AI 模型判断下一批可能被调用的数据页。系统会在应用真正发起请求前，先把相关数据从闪存迁回 DRAM，从而尽量维持主内存级别的访问体验。对云服务商和企业客户而言，这项技术可以提高现有服务器和集群的内存利用率，减少 DRAM 闲置，另一方面也能降低扩容时对高价 DRAM 的依赖，进而压缩总体拥有成本。

items.title: 项目负责人揭秘为何苹果 AI 版 Siri 姗姗来迟：推倒重来，彻底重构
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，iOS 27 全新人工智能版 Siri 相比旧版 Siri 实现了跨越式升级，但其上线耗时远超预期。迈克 · 罗克韦尔道出了新 Siri 迟迟未能推出的背后原因。IT之家注意到，苹果全球开发者大会（WWDC）主题演讲结束后的上周，苹果面向媒体举办了一场聚焦 Siri 与苹果智能（Apple Intelligence）的技术分享会。迈克 · 罗克韦尔也出席了本次分享会，他从去年才开始接手 Siri 项目的整体管理工作。罗克韦尔补充了全新细节，解释这款人工智能 Siri 的正式推出为何比预期更晚，以下是他的原话：去年我们其实已经做出了首个迭代版本，它是在原有 Siri 基础上小幅改良，新增了工具调用功能，并且能够正常运行。但我们认为，这款版本无法达到我们预想的产品愿景与使用体验。我们同时还有另一套设计方案，这套方案需要进行大规模底层重构，最终我们决定采用这套方案。于是我们推倒重来，完完整整从零重构 Siri。毫不夸张地说，是彻底拆解原有架构，依托阿马尔刚才介绍的顶尖大模型，从头搭建全新系统。这次重构让我们打造出能力实现质的飞跃的 Siri。新版 Siri 拥有独立应用程序，原生支持多模态交互，隐私保护设计贯穿底层架构。同时该功能覆盖苹果全平台，这一点对我们至关重要。iPhone、iPad、Mac、Apple Watch、Vision Pro，还有车载 C

items.title: macOS 26.4 为何拦截部分终端命令？苹果解释背后安全触发机制
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，苹果公司昨日（6 月 15 日）更新支持文档，解释称在 macOS 26.4 系统中，若用户不常用终端（Terminal），且命令来自网站、聊天智能体、消息或邮件应用，系统可能阻止粘贴。科技媒体 9to5Mac 指出，用户此前终端里粘贴命令后，系统会先给出安全警告，提示内容可能含有恶意代码。很多人只知道有这个拦截机制，但不清楚它的触发机制。在最新官方支持文档中，苹果公司表示如果用户并不经常使用终端，且复制的命令来自网站、聊天智能体、消息应用或邮件应用，系统就可能阻止粘贴。IT之家附上相关截图如下：苹果还补充了“已检测到恶意软件，已阻止粘贴”和“已阻止恶意脚本”两项提示，不再提供继续粘贴的选项，明确告知用户系统已识别命令或脚本中含有已知恶意代码。如果用户认为拦截是误判，尤其是命令访问的网站被错误标记为欺骗性页面，苹果建议前往 websitereview.apple.com 提交错误报告。参考If your Mac blocks a Terminal command paste or script

items.title: 招商银行推出“运通工程师信用卡”，新用户办卡提供“专属 AI 权益”单月可享 18 亿 Token M3 用量
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，招商银行宣布推出一款“运通工程师信用卡”，强调相应信用卡拥有“专属 AI 权益”。IT之家参考官方介绍获悉，新用户办卡首次参与活动达标后，至高可享每月 18 亿 Token M3 用量，可直接用于文档、图像、音视频等多模态模型调用，以及 MaxClaw 龙虾部署等 AI 高频场景。具体来看，相应信用卡“AI 权益体系”提供三种方案可选：面向频繁使用 AI 工具的专业开发者，提供 1 个月 MiniMax Token Plan Max，享受每月 18 亿 Token M3 用量，支持 4-5 个 Agent 并发运行；面向日常工作需求的个人开发者，提供 2 个月 MiniMax Token Plan Plus，享受每月 6 亿 Token M3 用量，支持 3-4 个 Agent 并发运行；面向需求更多元的 AI 使用者，提供 1 个月 MiniMax Token Plan Plus+1 个月 MaxClaw 基础版，在前者服务基础上，可以部署 7x24 小时在线的云端 MaxClaw，并在高峰时段优先使用。事实上，此前 Kimi 智能助手也推出了一款“Kimi 信用卡”，持卡人可通过消费积分兑换 Kimi 算力额度（如 Agent 使用额度、高级功能权限等），该卡预计 7 月正式面市。参考央行发布的《2025 年支付体系运行总体情况》，截至 2025

items.title: 古尔曼：苹果有望推出 AI 智能体，让 Siri 自主操作 iPhone 和 Mac 软件
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 16 日消息，彭博社记者马克 · 古尔曼认为，苹果最终可能推出一款产品，直接对标 OpenClaw—— 这是一套智能体 AI 系统，能够代用户自主操作各类软件。古尔曼在其专栏《Power On》中撰文表示，他预计苹果会研发一套系统，可全权代表用户操作 iPhone、iPad 与 Mac 端的各类软件。这一预测的依据，是苹果 Siri 工程主管迈克 · 罗克韦尔在上周苹果全球开发者大会（WWDC）主题演讲结束后发表的相关言论。罗克韦尔的表态似乎为 Siri 突破现有功能上限留下了空间。他将支撑这款语音助手的全新底层引擎称作一套“完全现代化的架构”，设计之初便充分考虑了功能拓展性：智能体（Agent）的运行逻辑是持续接收信息、做出判断、继而执行操作，形成循环。而目前我们的 Siri 仍主要依靠用户主动发起指令运行。但支撑 Siri 的底层架构已是全新现代化架构，因此我们未来拓展相关能力的空间十分充足。苹果软件工程高级副总裁克雷格 · 费德里吉也承认了智能体这一新兴赛道，但表述措辞十分审慎。他称该领域尚处于试验阶段，打造适配的用户体验仍是首要目标，同时并未完全排除苹果未来入局该领域的可能性。据IT之家了解，苹果即将上线的新版 Siri 已基于大语言模型完成重构，现阶段依旧是依托用户指令触发的交互系统。

items.title: 消息称三星晶圆代工首获马斯克Neuralink芯片制造订单
items.summary: 据报道，三星电子的晶圆代工业务首度获得马斯克旗下脑机接口企业Neuralink的芯片合同制造服务订单。三星晶圆代工将为Neuralink生产其“第四代”芯片，目标2027年底量产。该产品将采用4nm工艺制程，试产工作已于2026年5月启动。（财联社）

items.title: 银河证券：AI算力建设需求爆发推动存储长周期涨价，HBM投片比重将持续提升
items.summary: 36氪获悉，银河证券发布电子行业2026年中期策略称，AI算力建设需求爆发推动存储长周期涨价，HBM投片比重将持续提升。随着NVIDIA新一代Rubin Ultra平台即将推出，单颗GPU配置HBM容量将进一步提升至384GB，加上AI ASIC出货量持续增加，将带动HBM需求再度跃升。TrendForce预估，HBM投片量占整体DRAM投片比重将由2025年的18％提升至2027年约30％；HBM位元供给占比也将由8％提高至约13％。此外，随着端侧AI技术的爆发式增长，3D堆叠DRAM方案正成为突破传统存储瓶颈的关键技术路径。

items.title: 扬杰科技：拟变更部分募集资金投资项目
items.summary: 36氪获悉，扬杰科技公告，鉴于公司越南工厂一期已满产，海外研发中心、渠道均已布局到位，为切实提高募集资金使用效率，公司拟将2023年发行的全球存托凭证募集资金投资项目“发展功率元件业务，包括建设小信号产品、硅基及碳化硅SBD、MOSFET等产品的封装”项目、“海外研发中心和全球销售及售后服务网点建设”项目结项，剩余的募集资金用于公司正在实施的投资项目“车规级功率半导体模块封装项目”和“AI基础设施用功率器件生产线技术改造项目”。变更用途的募集资金截至2026年5月31日共计1.25亿美元（含利息和现金管理收益）。

items.summary: arXiv:2606.13682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The open shop scheduling problem (OSSP) arises in many industrial and service settings but remains computationally challenging as the number of jobs and machines increases. While exact methods quickly become intractable, classical dispatching rules and metaheuristics may require substantial tuning to maintain solution quality at large scales. This study develops a Transformer-based scheduling policy for OSSP using an encoder-decoder architecture with multi-head attention. items.title: UP-NRPA: User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation for Planning with Large Language Models in Goal-oriented Dialogue Systems
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To address the challenge that current dialogue policy planning methods struggle to dynamically adapt to diverse user characteristics, this paper proposes a User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation (UP-NRPA) online framework with Large Language Models. In contrast to conventional approaches dependent on model training and require offline reinforcement learning policy models for user groups, UP-NRPA enables dynamic customization of dialogue strategies through an adaptive mechanism. This is achieved by leveraging real-time user feedback

items.title: History of the Muddy Children Puzzle
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13703v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Muddy Children Puzzle is a puzzle about knowledge and ignorance that has been inspiring for the development of epistemic logic. Who came up with it first? This is unclear. We trace the origin of the Muddy Children Puzzle through logical and literary publications over the past two centuries. The puzzle inspired a numerous variations such as involving numbers or coloured hats. We also present a novel hats puzzle involving self-reference.

items.title: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent success of agent swarms has shifted the paradigm of large language model (LLM)-based agents from single-agent workflows to multi-agent systems, highlighting the importance of agent orchestration for task decomposition and collaboration. However, existing orchestration frameworks are limited to a narrow set of modalities and struggle to generalize to more complex settings where heterogeneous modalities coexist and interact. items.title: Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution Makes Better Deep Researcher
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13710v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep research and agent evolution serve as de-facto tasks for AI agents in real-world applications toward artificial general intelligence. The former enables autonomous retrieval and integration of information in open-ended environments to tackle open-ended research tasks, yet it is constrained by the static parametric deep research capabilities of agent systems. items.title: WorkBench Revisited: Workplace Agents Two Years On
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The best agent on WorkBench in March 2024, GPT-4, completed 43% of tasks and took an unintended harmful action, such as emailing the wrong person, on 26% of them. We re-visit the benchmark in June 2026 and find that the best agent to date, Claude Opus 4.8, completes 89% and takes an unintended harmful action on 2.5%. Aside from this considerable progress in frontier agent performance, three things stand out. items.title: Refusal Beyond a Single Direction: A Preliminary Comparison of Diff-in-Means and INLP
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arditi et al. (2024) has shown that refusal in safety fine-tuned chat models is mediated by a single linear direction in the residual stream, recoverable by a difference-in-means (DiM) of harmful and harmless activations. items.title: YeasierAgent: Agentic Social Sandbox as a Canvas for Intent-Driven Creation of Platform-Agnostic Symbiotic Agent-Native Applications
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces YeasierAgent, an application-building paradigm based on symbiotic agents, narrative worlds, and scene-aware interaction. It challenges the conventional device-coupled model of software by redefining applications as collaborative spaces among users, agents, and worlds. items.title: TwinBI: An Agentic Digital Twin for Efficient Augmented Interactions with Business Intelligence Dashboards
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13731v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Business intelligence (BI) increasingly combines dashboard interaction with LLM-based assistance, but these two modes often fall out of sync during multi-step analysis. As users switch between direct dashboard manipulation and natural-language queries, it becomes difficult to preserve a consistent analytical state across filters, hierarchies, metrics, and chart context. items.title: When Sample Selection Bias Precipitates Model Collapse
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13732v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The proliferation of recursive training on synthetic data can alleviate data scarcity but risks model collapse, where repeated training erodes distributional tails and homogenizes outputs. Data selection is widely viewed as a remedy, yet its reliability depends critically on the reference distribution used by the verifier. We show that in low-resource verification regimes, where each verifier observes only a small, fragmented, and biased slice of the target manifold, selection itself becomes biased. items.title: AI Receptivity or AI Adoption Breadth? A Tool-Specific Reanalysis of the Lower-Literacy/Higher-Usage Link
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent evidence reported by Tully, Longoni, and Appel (2025) suggests that lower artificial intelligence (AI) literacy predicts greater receptivity toward AI. We revisit this claim using the public data from Study 3 of that article, which measures past usage of five AI tool categories on a five-point frequency scale. items.title: MA-ProofBench: A Two-Tiered Evaluation of LLMs for Theorem Proving in Mathematical Analysis
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have made notable progress in automated theorem proving, yet existing formal benchmarks remain limited in both mathematical coverage and difficulty. Most are concentrated in areas that are easier to formalize, such as algebra and elementary number theory, and provide limited coverage of subfields that require deeper reasoning, including mathematical analysis. items.title: Poker Arena: Multi-Axis Profiling of Strategic Reasoning and Memory in LLMs
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13815v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Strategic reasoning under uncertainty underpins consequential decisions in negotiation, finance, and policy, but prevailing game-play benchmarks collapse heterogeneous reasoning dimensions into a single scalar, leaving the capability structure of frontier LLMs unexamined. items.title: Hyperdimensional computing for structured querying on tabular data embeddings
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13871v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tabular data embeddings have become a cornerstone of data profiling and data integration pipelines, enabling tasks such as entity annotation and resolution; schema matching; column type detection; and table search, among others. Existing approaches embed rows, columns, or entire tables into a vector space and rely on nearest-neighbor search to retrieve candidate matches. items.title: Capability Minimization as a Safety Primitive: Risk-Aware Causal Gating for Least-Privilege LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13884v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern decision systems increasingly rely on learned components whose outputs may be confident yet wrong, exposing downstream actions to costly errors. We introduce Risk-Aware Causal Gating (RACG), a framework that decides whether to act on, defer, or abstain from a model's prediction by combining causal effect estimation with calibrated risk control. items.title: A Multi-Agent AI System for Automated High School Transcript Processing: Collaborative Document Analysis at Scale
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13916v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Each year, college admissions offices face an overwhelming challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts, each with unique formats, grading systems, and layouts. This manual process creates operational bottlenecks that delay admissions decisions and consume valuable resources. items.title: Sorries Are Not the Hard Part: An Expert-Review Case Study of a Semi-Autonomous Formalization
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13925v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models can often close proof gaps in interactive theorem provers, but a verified theorem is not the same thing as a reusable library contribution. We study this distinction through a detailed case study: a semi-autonomous formalization of Grothendieck's vanishing theorem. items.title: Adversarial Concept Search: Predicting Compositional Errors From Feature Geometry
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13934v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humans cannot always intuit what scenarios are most challenging to LLMs. Hoping to capture challenging edge cases, developers either design problems to be difficult for humans or curate extensive benchmarks. What if we could instead anticipate which scenarios a model will fail on? In this paper, we use an LLM's representational geometry to predict which concept combinations it will fail on. items.title: Minim: Privacy-Aware Minimal View for Agents via Trusted Local Sanitization
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern LLM-powered autonomous agents increasingly rely on rich user interface (UI) state observations to achieve reliable action grounding in complex digital environments. However, many deployments transmit the full UI state to remote inference servers even when most elements are irrelevant to the current task, which can leak sensitive but unnecessary context such as authentication codes, private notifications, and background application states. items.title: Formalizing Numerical Analysis: An Agent Pipeline and Quality Audit Beyond Kernel Acceptance
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14000v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has demonstrated that coding agents can formalize entire advanced mathematics textbooks in Lean 4, yet existing efforts concentrate on branches of mathematics already well-represented in mathlib and measure success solely through kernel acceptance. items.title: Applicability Condition Extraction for Therapeutic Drug-Disease Relations
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Identifying conditions that a certain drug takes therapeutic effect on a target disease is crucial for clinical decision-making support. However, most existing biomedical information extraction methods have focused on identifying only relations between drugs and diseases, while largely overlooking the context-specific conditions where such relations can apply. items.title: FactoryLLM: A Safe and Open-Source AI Playground for Evaluating LLMs in Smart Factories
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnostics and recovery in smart factories is challenging because critical information is dispersed across manuals of multiple machines which are interconnected through the manufacturing process. Large Language Models (LLMs) can provide a promising approach. items.title: VeriGeo: Controllable Geometry Question Generation with Numerical and Analytical Verification
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometry problem generation is useful for AI-assisted education and multimodal mathematical reasoning, but reliable synthesis remains difficult because the problem statement, diagram, constraints, and solution should be mutually consistent. Existing methods often trade off controllability and reliability: seed-based rewriting is flexible but weakly verifiable, whereas diagram-first construction improves validity but is less suited to arbitrary user-specified constraints. items.title: When Should Agent Trust Be Conditional? Characterizing and Attacking Skill-Conditional Reputation in Agent Swarms
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Open platforms increasingly route tasks among heterogeneous LLM agents--differing in base model, scaffold, and tool stack--whose competence varies sharply by skill: an agent excellent at one skill may be useless at another. The standard reputation approach summarizes each agent by a single global trust score, but that scalar is the wrong object here, because routing every task to the globally most-trusted agent leaves the value of specialization unclaimed. items.title: Closing the Reflection Gap: A Free Calibration Bonus for Agentic RL
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed as agents that interact with external environments and observe feedback such as execution results, error messages, and tool outputs. A well-functioning agent should be able to leverage this feedback to accurately assess its own performance. Yet we find a persistent reflection gap: LLM agents tend to mis-assess their own outputs after observing concrete environment feedback -- even for questions they correctly answered -- and standard RL barely helps due to a credit-assignment mismatch. items.title: SkillAudit: Ground-Truth-Free Skill Evolution via Paired Trajectory Auditing
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent skills are structured procedural packages that guide frozen LLM agents in specialized workflows. Skills rarely remain sufficient after deployment: edge cases, API changes, and deployment constraints become visible only through use, making skill evolution a practical necessity. items.title: AFFORDANCE20Q: Evaluating Affordance Reasoning from Physical Properties
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Affordance reasoning, the inference of an object's action possibilities from its physical properties (e.g., shape and material), is fundamental to human physical understanding and increasingly critical for Large Language Models (LLMs). However, existing affordance benchmarks largely expose explicit object identities in the evaluation setup, allowing models to rely on memorized object-affordance mappings rather than reasoning over physical properties. items.title: HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agent performance depends critically on the runtime harness, comprising the prompts, tools, memory, and control flow that mediate how a model observes, reasons, and acts. Yet today's harnesses remain largely hand-crafted and static: each new model or task still demands bespoke scaffolding, and the rich traces produced during execution are rarely distilled back into systematic improvement. items.title: Communication Policy Evolution for Proactive LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14314v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents have rapidly evolved into autonomous systems, yet a persistent information gap remains between users and agents: communication is costly, while users' identical preferences further limit information exchange. To investigate how agents should communicate across modalities, this paper formalizes Communication Policy, establishes textual and UI-based policies, and then evaluates communication policies across diverse environments, personas, and model combinations. items.title: CSPO: Constraint-Sensitive Policy Optimization for Safe Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14415v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (Safe RL) aims to maximize expected return while satisfying safety constraints, typically modeled as Constrained Markov Decision Processes (CMDPs). While primal-dual methods scale well to deep RL, they often suffer from delayed constraint correction, leading to oscillatory behavior and prolonged safety violations. items.title: Causal Object-Centric Models for Planning with Monte Carlo Tree Search
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14418v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce COMET (Causal Object-centric Model for Efficient Tree search), a model-based reinforcement learning algorithm that performs Monte Carlo Tree Search in a slot-structured latent space. items.title: GitOfThoughts: Version-Controlled Reasoning and Agent Memory You Can Replay, Diff, and Merge
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14470v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) reasoning is ephemeral: chains of thought vanish with the context window, pruned search branches leave no record, and memory buffers cannot be diffed, merged, or audited. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14476v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A growing line of work equips large language model (LLM) agents with graph neural networks (GNNs) as callable tools, assuming the agent exercises judgment over when and how much to rely on such a tool. items.title: From Chatbot to Digital Colleague: The Paradigm Shift Toward Persistent Autonomous AI
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14502v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are undergoing a fundamental transformation from conversational generators into integrated AI systems capable of reasoning, action, memory, and self-improvement. items.title: Dense Coordinate-List Fine-Tuning Induces a Controllable Interference Surface in Vision-Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fine-tuning vision-language models to emit dense coordinate lists improves visual grounding but also changes how models serialize, repeat, and terminate structured outputs. items.title: Every Eval Ever: A Unifying Schema and Community Repository for AI Evaluation Results
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI evaluations are widely used for testing and understanding progress. First, results are saved in incompatible formats, scattered across leaderboards, papers, blog posts, evaluation harness logs, and custom repositories. items.title: StreamMemBench: Streaming Evaluation of Agent Memory for Future-Oriented Assistance
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central role of personal-agent memory is to turn stored information and prior interactions into future-oriented assistance. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14579v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When applying Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for GUI Grounding, rollouts are sampled from a single screenshot view; groups often become either all failures on difficult instances or all successes on easy ones, yielding no useful relative advantage. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14582v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Efficient route optimization play a vital role in ensuring both safety and punctuality in railway operations.
